The main purpose in these books of Chronicles seems to be to keep records of David’s family line. Therefore, the writer does not repeat the history of Saul's reign but only mentions his death, by which a way was made for David to the throne. From Saul’s downfall, we may learn: 1. That the sin of sinners will certainly find them out, sooner or later; Saul died because of his transgression. 2. That no one's greatness can exempt him from God's judgments. 3. Disobedience is destructive; Saul died for not keeping the word of the Lord. May we be delivered from unbelief, impatience, and despair. By waiting on the Lord we will obtain a kingdom that cannot be moved.
